title: Smoky Zesty Australian Callaloo Stew
description: A citrusy and smoky stew featuring fresh callaloo, perfect for a sick day and compliant with a Jain diet.
instructions: Wash and roughly chop 600g of callaloo leaves, discarding thick stems.
In a large pot, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium heat.
Add smoked paprika, sea salt, black pepper, and red chili flakes; stir for 30 seconds.
Pour in 4 cups vegetable broth and 1 cup water, bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the chopped callaloo, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until wilted.
Add coconut milk and the juice of one lime, stir well.
Stir in the juice of one orange, adjust seasoning with more salt or pepper if needed.
Reduce heat to low and let the stew thicken for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust flavors, adding extra lime juice if acidity is desired.
Ladle the stew into bowls, garnish with a sprinkle of smoked paprika if desired, and serve hot.
